summary: Added the coin item implementation in src/melee/it/items/itcoin.c with its state table, behavior callbacks, spawn helper it_802F2094, and supporting item variable/attribute structs and prototypes. The PR also corrected related signatures, notably making it_802F2094 and Ground_801C58E0 return Item_GObj* instead of s32, and marked many itcoin-local functions and sdata2 constants local in symbols.txt. Review focused on header hygiene and type conventions: prefer int or bool over s32 where appropriate, prefer UNK_T over M2C_UNK, and ensure new headers have include guards because missing guards break m2ctx.
what_changed: Created a 429-line src/melee/it/items/itcoin.c implementation for the coin item, including static state callbacks, the ItemStateTable it_803F93C8, visible/global functions such as it_802F13B4, it_802F14E4, it_802F1518, it_802F1888, it_802F18F0, it_802F1910, it_802F1CE0, it_802F1F48, it_802F2014, it_802F2020, it_802F202C, and spawn helper it_802F2094.
Added coin-specific data layouts in itCommonItems.h: itCoinAttributes with float fields x0 through x48 and CoinVars with integer and float fields used under Item.xDD4_itemVar.coin.

Updated it_802F2094 prototype in it_27CF.h from s32 return and s32 parameters to Item_GObj* it_802F2094(int, Vec3*, int, int), matching its role as a coin spawn function returning the created item gobj.
Changed Ground_801C58E0 in ground.c and ground.h from returning s32 to returning Item_GObj*, because it forwards the result of it_802F2094.
Added external prototypes needed by itcoin, including gm_8017E280, grFigureGet_80219C50, grFigureGet_80219C34, it_8027B798, it_8027CBFC, and un_8031C5E4.
Marked many itcoin helper functions in config/GALE01/symbols.txt as local rather than global, matching their static definitions in itcoin.c.
Marked several itcoin sdata2 constants from it_804DD9B8 through it_804DD9D0 as local in symbols.txt.
Touched Item struct typing in src/melee/it/types.h, with the PR notes saying this was driven by accesses in it_802F1F48.
smart_moves: Kept internal itcoin state callbacks static and synchronized that choice with symbols.txt scope changes, reducing exported symbol noise.
Propagated the real return type of it_802F2094 through Ground_801C58E0 instead of leaving the spawn result as an integer placeholder.
Introduced explicit CoinVars and itCoinAttributes structs before using the xDD4 item variable fields heavily, giving the decompilation a stable place to encode observed item-specific layout.
Used existing item-state helper patterns such as Item_80268E5C and ItemStateTable entries instead of inventing a new dispatch mechanism.
Added small dependent prototypes across gm, ground/stage, display, and item headers so the new translation unit could compile with fewer implicit/placeholder declarations.
decomp_lessons: When a newly decompiled function reveals a callee returns a game object pointer, update the callee prototype and all simple forwarding wrappers together; this PR did that for it_802F2094 and Ground_801C58E0.
Static item state callbacks should generally be reflected as local scope in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Item-specific unions or variable blocks should be named and typed once in common item headers when multiple item functions access the same offsets.
Header additions must be m2ctx-safe: include guards are not optional for new headers.
Prefer project-wide placeholder conventions in headers: review explicitly requested UNK_T over M2C_UNK and int or bool over s32 where appropriate.

The author noted weak spots honestly: it_802F1630 and it_802F1CE0 were suspected non-matching for the same reason, and it_802F1910 was close but differed in the first if condition. Future work should inspect those exact control-flow/codegen issues rather than assuming they were fully solved here.
naming_conventions: Coin item functions retained address-based names such as it_802F13B4 and it_802F2094 because no semantic names were established in the evidence.
Coin-specific structs were named itCoinAttributes and CoinVars, following existing item-specific attribute/variable struct style while leaving unknown fields as offset names.
Internal callbacks in itcoin.c were declared static and kept with address comments like /* 2F1588 */.
Reviewer guidance: use int or bool instead of s32 for ordinary integer/boolean APIs when possible.
Reviewer guidance: use UNK_T instead of M2C_UNK for unknown types in headers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in functions such as it_802F1630, it_802F1910, and it_802F1CE0 to influence stack layout for matching.
Encoded item state callback arrays with exact function pointer ordering, including NULL where no callback was present.
Changed symbol scope from global to local for static helper functions and local constants to align linker metadata with C storage class.
Adjusted prototype return types to pointer types to improve type propagation and avoid treating item gobj results as integers.

The PR notes identify specific matching pain points: Ground_801C58E0 changed because it returns it_802F2094; struct Item changes were driven by accesses in it_802F1F48; it_802F1630 and it_802F1CE0 appeared non-matching for a shared reason; it_802F1910 was nearly matching except for a first-if condition.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Use int or bool instead of s32 in general, and use UNK_T in place of M2C_UNK.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: New header needed an include guard; missing include guards cause m2ctx to break. gen_header from tools/functions.sh can generate one.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested structure: #ifndef __TYDISPLAY_H / #define __TYDISPLAY_H, include <platform.h>, declare void* un_8031C5E4(s32), then #endif.

review_feedback.author: wyatt-avilla
review_feedback.feedback: Author acknowledged the include guard issue: 'oops, thanks for catching that'.

follow_up_queries: Inspect final merged src/melee/ty/tydisplay.h to confirm the include guard and include order were corrected as suggested.
Revisit it_802F1630 and it_802F1CE0 to identify the shared non-matching cause mentioned by the author.
Revisit it_802F1910 first-if condition and compare against assembly to determine the exact boolean/control-flow form required.
Audit other wrappers around item spawn functions for s32 return types that should be Item_GObj*.
Search for remaining M2C_UNK uses in headers touched by item decompilation and consider replacing with UNK_T where consistent with review guidance.
Investigate whether fields in itCoinAttributes and CoinVars can now be semantically named from coin behavior.
